channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Stay tuned for the Monthly Report for April next week!!! :D
Mastering Your Frontend Build with Bazel: Consolidating Tests
item.description: In my previous post I consolidated the lint setup across all 7 packages in the Pedalboard monorepo. The key move there was introducing tools/lint/linters.bzl, a shared macro file that wraps...

item.title: Stop Wasting GitHub Actions Minutes: How We Built a Commit-Driven CI System for iOS
item.description: If you're building an iOS app with GitHub Actions, you're probably burning through macOS runner minutes like they're free. Spoiler: they're not — macOS runners cost 10x more than Linux runners,...

item.title: Claude Code hooks: the half of Claude Code nobody uses
item.description: I was halfway through writing this post when I decided to fact-check myself. Opened ~/.claude/settings.json, expecting three or four hooks I'd forgotten about. There was one. A Stop hook that...

item.title: The End of the Prototype Trap: Real Engineering Lessons from Google Cloud Next '26
item.description: This is a submission for the Google Cloud NEXT Writing Challenge It is easy to get swept up in the polished keynote demos of new AI capabilities. However, when evaluating new technology, I...

item.title: GitOps Deployment for Kubernetes Teams
item.description: TLDR ; GitOps uses Git as the single source of truth for infrastructure, enabling auditable and repeatable deployments ArgoCD and Flux are the two leading GitOps tools, each suited to different...

item.title: Great news, the Moto G Stylus is no longer teeming with bloatware
item.description: The 2026 edition of Motorola's stylus phone is plenty appealing. My review unit is a charming lilac color, has a pleasantly textured back panel, and includes a MicroSD slot and what might be the...

item.title: OpenGraph, Twitter Cards, and article metadata in Astro
item.description: Original post: OpenGraph, Twitter Cards, and article metadata in Astro Series: Part of How this blog was built — documenting every decision that shaped this site. When someone shares a link to a...

item.title: Working with Artificial Intelligence: Risks & Side Effects
item.description: ℹ️ The texts in this article were partly generated by artificial intelligence and corrected and revised by us. In our last article on working with generative texts, we already pointed out initial...

item.title: Microsoft brings Xbox back, scraps Microsoft Gaming
item.description: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has had a busy week. Sharma held an internal town hall with Xbox employees today, just days after announcing an Xbox Game Pass price cut, commenting on the status of the Xbox...

item.title: Escape the cloud
item.description: A local-first platform where users own their data cryptographically — built on encrypted SQLite, CRDTs, and libp2p. Here is the simplest thing you can do with the platform: const platform = await...

item.title: Why Is Redis INCR a Bad Fit for a Public URL Shortener?
item.description: LeetDezine Redis INCR is one of those solutions that looks perfect the first time you see it. Atomic counter increments. Every call returns a unique integer. Base62-encode it and you have a short...

item.title: Keychron’s customizable V1 mechanical keyboard is back down to $45
item.description: If you’ve ever wished your laptop keyboard was better tailored to your workflow, the Keychron V1 is a solid upgrade. It doesn’t cost as much as most other mechanical keyboards, but it still offers...

item.title: Era raises $11M to build a software platform for AI gadgets
item.description: Era thinks that we will see many form factors of AI hardware, including glasses, rings, and pendants

item.title: Inside Microsoft’s wave of executive departures
item.description: It feels like not a week has gone by this year without a senior Microsoft executive leaving the company. Some departures have triggered sweeping shakeups of Microsoft's biggest businesses, while...

item.title: 25 years later, is it time for a new iPod?
item.description: This is Lowpass by Janko Roettgers, a newsletter on the ever-evolving intersection of tech and entertainment, syndicated just for The Verge subscribers once a week. This year marks the 25th...

item.title: Microsoft offers voluntary retirement to long-serving employees
item.description: Microsoft is changing up its annual rewards and performance programs today, and offering long-serving employees in the US the ability to voluntarily retire. It's the first time in Microsoft's more...

item.title: DJI’s new Lito Series beginner drones start at less than $400
item.description: Following teasers and leaks, DJI has officially announced its new entry-level Lito 1 and Lito X1 drones that both weigh less than 249 grams so they're not subjected to registration requirements in...

item.title: Netflix can’t seem to follow up its biggest shows
item.description: It hasn't been that long since Stranger Things ended, but even still fans are clearly clamoring for more. The finale was overshadowed by a conspiracy about a secret episode, and an otherwise...

item.title: Grab a ticket today: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 kicks off in just a week in San Francisco
item.description: StrictlyVC San Francisco is in just a week. Now’s the time to grab yourself a ticket. Join VCs and founders at Sentro Filipino Cultural Center on April 30.

item.title: Another customer of troubled startup Delve suffered a big security incident
item.description: TechCrunch has confirmed that Delve was the compliance company that performed the security certifications for Context AI, the AI agent training startup that last week disclosed a security incident.

item.title: THE PEOPLE DO NOT YEARN FOR AUTOMATION
item.description: Today on Decoder, I want to lay out an idea that’s been banging around my head for weeks now as we’ve been reporting on AI and having conversations here on this show. I’ve been calling it software...

item.title: You’re about to feel the AI money squeeze
item.description: Earlier this month, millions of OpenClaw users woke up to a sweeping mandate: The viral AI agent tool, which this year took the worldwide tech industry by storm, had been severely restricted by...

item.title: Bitwarden CLI Compromised in Ongoing Checkmarx Supply Chain Campaign
item.description: Bitwarden CLI has been compromised as part of the newly discovered and ongoing Checkmarx supply chain campaign, according to new findings from JFrog and Socket. "The affected package version...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: $290M DeFi Hack, macOS LotL Abuse, ProxySmart SIM Farms +25 New Stories
item.description: You scroll past one incident and see another that feels familiar, like it should have been fixed years ago, but it still works with small changes. Same bugs. Same mistakes. The supply chain is...

item.title: AI galaxy hunters are adding to the global GPU crunch
item.description: Astronomers are turning to GPUs to find needles in the galactic haystack.

item.title: The Download: introducing the Nature issue
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Introducing: the Nature issue When we talk about “nature,”...

item.title: [Webinar] Mythos Reality Check: Beating Automated Exploitation at AI Speed
item.description: Imagine a world where hackers don't sleep, don't take breaks, and find weak spots in your systems instantly. Well, that world is already here. Thanks to AI, attackers are now launching automated,...

item.title: Beehiiv rolls out new creator tools, including webinars and customizable paywalls
item.description: The announcement is a clear sign the company is trying to become an all-in-one hub for creators, reducing the hassle of juggling various tools and services to run their businesses.

item.title: Here’s how our TPUs power increasingly demanding AI workloads.
item.description: Learn how Google’s TPUs power increasingly demanding AI workloads with this new video.
